Python Full Course · Day 3

Variables &
Data Types

The Foundation of Every Python Program

▶  Start Learning Live Code Demo
↓
01 · Variables

What is a Variable?

A variable is a named container that stores data in your program's memory — like a labelled box.

🪣 Variable = Bucket
Data = Water stored inside
🪣

Bucket

Holds water — the bucket is the variable, water is the value

water = "H2O"
📦

Labelled Box

A box with a label. The label is the name, contents are the value

box = "books"
🏷️

Name Tag

Like a name badge — it tells you what's being referred to

name = "Alex"

Think of a variable as a labelled container in your computer's memory.

When you write:

name = "Thoufiq"
age = 21
city = "Chennai"

You're creating three containers:

📦 name → holds the text "Thoufiq"
📦 age → holds the number 21
📦 city → holds the text "Chennai"

Later in the program, just use the name to get the value back instantly!

variables.py
1name = "Thoufiq"
2age = 21
3city = "Chennai"
4
5print(name)
6print(age)
7print(city)
▶ OUTPUT
Thoufiq
21
Chennai
02 · Purpose

Why Use Variables?

💾

Store Data

Keep any piece of information — text, numbers, decisions — ready to use whenever needed.

♻️

Reuse Data

Write a value once, use it a hundred times. No need to repeat yourself.

✏️

Change Values Easily

Update one variable and the change spreads everywhere it's used instantly.

🔧

Flexible Programs

Variables make your code dynamic — it adapts to different inputs and situations.

03 · Rules

Variable Naming Rules

✅ Valid Names

✓
student_nameunderscore OK
✓
agesimple word
✓
salary123digits at end OK
✓
_privatestarts with _
✓
myScorecamelCase fine

❌ Invalid Names

✗
1namestarts with number
✗
student-namehyphen not allowed
✗
classreserved keyword
✗
my scorespaces not allowed
✗
@namespecial chars
R1

Must start with a letter (a–z, A–Z) or underscore _

R2

Cannot start with a number — digits are only allowed after the first character

R3

No spaces — use underscore instead: first_name not first name

R4

Python is case-sensitive — Name and name are two different variables

04 · Data Types

Python Data Types

A data type tells Python what kind of data is stored in a variable. Different types behave differently.

strText — names, sentences, words"Hello"
intWhole numbers — age, count, score21
floatDecimal numbers — CGPA, price, height8.75
boolTrue or False — yes/no decisionsTrue
datatypes_overview.py
1# String – text in quotes
2name = "Thoufiq"
3
4# Integer – whole number
5age = 21
6
7# Float – decimal number
8cgpa = 8.75
9
10# Boolean – True or False
11is_student = True
str · String

String str

type: str

A String stores text. It must be wrapped in single or double quotes. Strings are used for names, messages, addresses — any textual information.

Think of it like a 📝 message written on paper — it's readable text.

👤 Names 📍 Addresses 💬 Messages 📧 Emails
string_type.py
1channel = "Tech with Thoufiq"
2city = "Chennai"
3greeting = "Hello, World!"
4
5print(channel)
6print(city)
7print(greeting)
▶ OUTPUT
Tech with Thoufiq
Chennai
Hello, World!
int · Integer

Integer int

type: int

An Integer stores whole numbers — positive, negative, or zero. No decimals. Used for counting things, ages, scores, ranks.

Think of it like 🔢 counting apples — you can have 5 apples, not 5.7 apples.

🎂 Age 🏆 Score 📦 Count 📅 Year
integer_type.py
1age = 21
2subscribers = 5000
3videos = 30
4
5print(age)
6print(subscribers)
7print(videos)
▶ OUTPUT
21
5000
30
float · Decimal

Float float

type: float

A Float stores decimal numbers. Used whenever you need precision — prices, measurements, grades, coordinates.

Think of it like a 📏 ruler — it can measure 8.5 cm, not just 8 or 9.

📐 CGPA 💰 Price ⚖️ Weight 🌡️ Temp
float_type.py
1cgpa = 8.75
2price = 299.99
3height = 5.9
4
5print(cgpa)
6print(price)
7print(height)
▶ OUTPUT
8.75
299.99
5.9
bool · Boolean

Boolean bool

type: bool

A Boolean stores only two values: True or False. Used for yes/no decisions — is the user logged in? Is the student placed?

Think of it like a 💡 light switch — it's either ON (True) or OFF (False). Nothing in between.

🔐 Login ✅ Placed 💡 Toggle 🔔 Active
ON
True
OFF
False
boolean_type.py
1is_student = True
2is_placed = False
3is_logged_in = True
4
5print(is_student)
6print(is_placed)
▶ OUTPUT
True
False
05 · Built-in Function

The type() Function

Use type() to check what data type is stored in any variable.

type("Thoufiq")→<class 'str'>
type(21)→<class 'int'>
type(8.75)→<class 'float'>
type(True)→<class 'bool'>
check_types.py
1name = "Thoufiq"
2age = 21
3cgpa = 8.5
4is_student = True
5
6print(type(name))
7print(type(age))
8print(type(cgpa))
9print(type(is_student))
▶ OUTPUT
<class 'str'>
<class 'int'>
<class 'float'>
<class 'bool'>
06 · Real World

Real World Example

This is how variables are used in a real student management application

👤
student_name = "Alex"
str — stores the student's name
🎂
age = 20
int — stores whole number age
📐
cgpa = 8.5
float — stores decimal grade point
💼
placed = False
bool — placed or not yet
student_app.py
1# Student Record Application
2student_name = "Alex"
3age = 20
4cgpa = 8.5
5placed = False
6
7print(student_name)
8print(age)
9print(cgpa)
10print(placed)
▶ OUTPUT
Alex
20
8.5
False

VS Code Demo

Putting it all together — here's the complete code from today's VS Code session

day3_variables.py — Tech With Thoufiq
📁
🔍
🔀
🐛
🧩
1# Day 3 – Variables and Data Types
2# Tech with Thoufiq
3
4name = "Thoufiq" # str
5age = 21 # int
6cgpa = 8.5 # float
7is_student = True # bool
8
9print("Name:", name)
10print("Age:", age)
11print("CGPA:", cgpa)
12print("Student:", is_student)
13
14print(type(name))
15print(type(age))
16print(type(cgpa))
17print(type(is_student))
TERMINAL — OUTPUT
Name: Thoufiq
Age: 21
CGPA: 8.5
Student: True
<class 'str'>
<class 'int'>
<class 'float'>
<class 'bool'>
